name: MoneyGram
description_short: MoneyGram is a money transfer service with 61+ locations across Chicago offering domestic and international transfers, bill payments, and money orders.
description_long:
MoneyGram operates as a global money transfer network with significant presence in Chicago, Illinois. The company maintains over 61 physical locations throughout the city, partnering with various local businesses including currency exchanges, pharmacies (CVS), and independent money services to provide convenient access to its services.

MoneyGram's core offering includes domestic money transfers, international money transfers, money orders, bill payment services, and check cashing capabilities. Customers can initiate and manage transfers both online and through in-person visits at partner locations. The service is designed to serve individuals who need quick access to funds or wish to send money to recipients without traditional banking relationships.

MoneyGram's distribution model through partner locations—including currency exchanges, pharmacy chains, and independent money services—distinguishes it from traditional banks by offering extended hours and neighborhood accessibility. Many locations operate early morning through late evening, with some open until 11:59 PM, accommodating customers outside standard banking hours. The company emphasizes online management capabilities alongside offline service.

As a money transfer facilitator rather than a lender, MoneyGram is best suited for consumers needing immediate fund transfers rather than credit products. Fees for transfers are not disclosed on the location page, and customers should verify specific service availability and pricing at their chosen location, as MoneyGram operates through independent agents whose fee structures may vary.
pros: 61+ locations across Chicago providing neighborhood accessibility
Extended operating hours—some locations open until 11:59 PM, others from 6:30 AM
Dual service model allowing online management or in-person transactions
Multiple partner types (currency exchanges, pharmacies, independent agents) for convenience
Serves unbanked and underbanked populations needing transfer services without checking accounts
Domestic and international transfer capabilities from single locations
cons: Fees for transfers not disclosed on website; customers must contact individual locations for pricing
Service dependent on partner locations' operating hours and reliability
No credit-building or financial services beyond money movement
Limited transparency about exchange rates for international transfers on location page
best_for: Unbanked individuals needing to send or receive money domestically or internationally
Customers in Chicago neighborhoods without convenient bank access
People requiring after-hours money transfer services
diagnosis: MoneyGram is best for unbanked or underbanked Chicago residents needing to send/receive domestic or international money transfers quickly through accessible neighborhood locations. The primary caveat is that fees and exchange rates vary by partner agent location, and customers must contact their chosen location directly for specific pricing and service confirmation.
services: Location-based service finder with map integration
